nativeRange.habitat: Shallow freshwater margins of streams, ponds, tidal rivers and estuarine flats; tolerant of brackish exposure in coastal habitats

summary: A grass-like rosette plant that spreads aggressively by runners to form a midground meadow; smaller, more cold-tolerant cousin of Vallisneria and a forgiving choice for beginner planted tanks.
careNotes: Sagittaria subulata is widely sold as 'Dwarf Sagittaria' and is generally considered one of the easiest grass-like plants in the hobby. It is most commonly recommended as a mid-ground plant, though under strong lighting it stays compact enough to function as a tall foreground/carpet; in dimmer setups the leaves can stretch to 30–50 cm and behave more like a background plant. Plant the crown just above the substrate — burying the crown is a common cause of die-back. Light demand is on the low-to-medium end across sources (Tropica rates it 'low'; Modern Aquarium and aqua-fish.net describe moderate light as ideal); the plant tolerates a wide range and tends to colour up with slight reddish-orange tones under more intense light. CO2 is not required (Tropica lists a medium target of 6–14 mg/L; most retail sources call CO2 optional); supplementation accelerates carpeting and densifies the runners. It is a fairly heavy root feeder and benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate and monthly root tabs. Temperature tolerance is broad (~20–28 °C reported across sources, with some carpeting reports up to 29 °C); the species is unusually cold-tolerant for a tropical aquarium plant and survives unheated tanks in temperate climates. pH 6.5–8.0 and gH 3–15 are both well tolerated, including hard-water African cichlid setups where many delicate plants fail. Propagation is by short stolons/runners — a healthy mother plant will send out new rosettes every few weeks and can quickly cover open substrate; thin or replant runners as needed to keep the layout tidy. Native to the eastern United States and parts of northern South America (Colombia, Venezuela); the wild range crosses freshwater into mildly brackish coastal habitats, which explains its salinity tolerance, but in the aquarium it is grown strictly as a freshwater plant.
breedingNotes: Propagates very readily by short stolons (runners) from the base of the mother plant; each runner produces a new rosette that can be separated and replanted once it has its own roots. Flowering and seed production are occasionally reported on long submerged or emergent stalks but are uncommon and not a meaningful propagation route in aquaria.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica species page for Sagittaria subulata. Origin South America, height 5–30+ cm (with occasional growth to ~50 cm), light demand low, CO2 demand medium (6–14 mg/L), growth rate medium, propagation by short stolons forming a compact group, recommended as foreground plant. Manifest-supplied search URL (tropica.com/en/search?q=Sagittaria+subulata) returned only the site footer; resolved the canonical plant-details page via web search.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed temperature 71–84 °F (~22–29 °C), moderate light preferred (tolerates lower), CO2 optional, runner propagation, foreground–midground placement, easy care level. Native range stated as 'coastal areas of the Eastern United States' (complements Tropica's 'South America' — the species actually occurs across both).

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked: grass-like carpeting/foreground plant, tolerant of hard water, beginner-friendly, height varies with light intensity (taller in lower light).

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Sagittaria subulata
additional.notes: Used to fill temperature (20–26 °C), pH (6.5–8.0), and hardness (8–18 °dGH) where Tropica/Modern Aquarium are silent. Confirms family Alismataceae, runner propagation, fine nutrient-rich gravel, easy difficulty. Cross-source spread on light demand (low vs medium): averaged to keep low–medium guidance; spread on height (5–30 cm Tropica vs 30–40 cm aqua-fish): averaged to 10–30 cm typical adult size.
